commit.message:
Add parallel subagents and scheduled autonomous agents

Parallel subagents:
- /obsidian-save spawns per-type agents (people, projects, tasks, decisions, ideas)
- /obsidian-health spawns per-category agents (links, duplicates, frontmatter, staleness, orphans)
- /obsidian-recap spawns one agent per daily note for parallel reading
- /obsidian-init spawns agents for dashboard, templates, boards, and note samples simultaneously

Scheduled autonomous agents:
- obsidian-morning: daily 8am — creates daily note, surfaces overdue tasks
- obsidian-nightly: daily 10pm — closes out the day, appends summary
- obsidian-weekly: Fridays 6pm — generates weekly review note
- obsidian-health-check: Sundays 9pm — runs health check, saves report only

README updated with new Features entries and Scheduled Agents section with setup instructions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: AuthGate was redirecting all patient tabs back to dashboard

The inAuthFlow check matched all /patient/* routes, so navigating to
Bookings, Records, Pharmacy, or Profile immediately triggered a redirect
back to the home tab. Now inAuthFlow only matches the splash screen and
login/OTP screens, and adds proper redirect-to-login for expired tokens
on protected routes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: handle Windows Terminal trailing whitespace stripping in paste events (#58)

Windows Terminal strips trailing whitespace from each line and trailing
newlines when forwarding clipboard content via bracketed paste. This
caused _on_paste to treat the paste as external, overwriting the local
clipboard (losing trailing spaces) and resetting the line-copy flag
(breaking VS Code line-paste behavior).

Use a trailing-whitespace-tolerant comparison (rstrip per line after
rstrip on the whole string) so that whitespace-only differences
preserve the local clipboard and line-copy state.

commit.message:
Overhaul scripts, add experiment tracking, and document organization

Scripts:
- Replace nemo_sif_exec/shell and vllm_v012_exec/shell with unified
  nemo_sif.sh and vllm_sif.sh (no-args = interactive shell, args = exec)
- Remove vllm patch file binds (patches didn't work)
- Remove old Option A pytorch-devel SIF scripts (build_sif, sif_exec,
  sif_shell, eval_slurm, train_slurm, install_deps, setup_and_eval)
- Update all references across SLURM scripts, Python files, and docs

Eval:
- Switch evaluation to vLLM offline inference (run_eval_vllm.py)
- Remove old run_eval.py (transformers-based, superseded)
- Auto-name preds files with SLURM_JOB_ID or timestamp prefix

Experiment tracking:
- Add --experiment flag to train.py, train_grpo.py, run_eval_vllm.py
- Prefixes all artifact names: nemotron-lora/<exp>_<job_id>/,
  preds/<exp>_<job_id>_*.json
- Auto-writes JSON manifest to experiments/<exp>/ on completion
  (adapter path, accuracy scores, config snapshot)
- Add experiments/ to .gitignore

Docs:
- Add CLAUDE.md with full architecture, common commands, experiment
  best practices, eval config reference, and project layout
- Add EXPERIMENTS.md and RESULTS.md (experiment log + results table)
- Add GRPO trainer (train_grpo.py), prompt strategy hints (prompts.py)
- Add configs: base.yaml updates, eval_subset.json, eval_subset_by_qtype.json

commit.message:
Add "gap" and "missing" to easy mode banned vocabulary, fix skill instructions

"Gap" is verdict language — "One additional gap was found" is the
opposite of easy mode's observational tone. The di-pr-review skill
used "gap" in its own instructions ("Identify the gap between..."),
training Claude to think in gap-framing. This leaked into PR
descriptions even when easy mode was active.

- docs/EASY_MODE_LANGUAGE.md: add "gap"/"gap was found"/"gap in
  coverage" and "missing" (as verdict) to the softening vocabulary
  table with observational alternatives
- skills/di-pr-review/SKILL.md: reword two instructions that used
  "gap" as framing (lines 3016, 4608)
- analyses/process-easy-mode-session-scope.md: filed postmortem

commit.message:
fix: derive streets from actual section elements, not grid formulas

Measure getBoundingClientRect() of each grid child, group into rows
by Y position, find real pixel gaps between section edges. Streets
are midpoints of actual spaces between windows. Handles expanded
sections gracefully — if a street disappears, ants remap to
available streets. No coordinate assumptions or grid math.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(metrics): guard Eio.Cancel and use atomic counter ops (#36)

Three fixes in metrics.ml:

1. server_interceptor: add Eio.Cancel.Cancelled guard so fiber
   cancellation does not inflate error counters. On cancel, only
   the active_calls gauge is decremented (cleanup) before re-raise.

2. Counter.inc / Counter.inc_by: replace non-atomic
   Atomic.set(Atomic.get + n) with Atomic.fetch_and_add to prevent
   lost increments under concurrent access.

3. serve_prometheus: narrow catch-all `| _ ->` to specific
   exceptions (Buffer_limit_exceeded, Failure) and re-raise
   Eio.Cancel.Cancelled.

commit.message:
perf: zombie system overhaul — smooth animation, optimized loop, no lag

Frontend:
- _animateMarkerTo with 4800ms duration for visible zombies (smooth walk)
- Off-screen zombies use instant setLatLng (no wasted animation)
- Viewport check via map.getBounds().contains()

Backend moveZombies:
- Early bail if no active hordes
- Pre-cache all player positions (one loop, not per-zombie)
- Dead zombies auto-deleted from Map on iteration (cleanup stale)
- Fast approximate distance (sqrt of degree diff) instead of haversine
- Attack events collected in batch array, emitted after move loop
- Removed markDirty('zombies') — positions not persisted

commit.message:
Switch transcript fetching from youtube-transcript to Supadata API

youtube-transcript fails on Vercel because YouTube blocks data center
IPs. Supadata proxies the request, fixing ingestion in production.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Ban "pre-existing" as a failure category, enforce correct taxonomy

Claude consistently labels failures as "probably pre-existing" when they
occur on platforms the PR doesn't target (macOS, JRuby, etc). This is
wrong — broken master is extremely rare in dd-trace-rb. The label is a
rationalization to skip investigation.

Replace the vague "never assume unrelated" rule with a concrete taxonomy:
- Infrastructure failure: log matches detection script → restart
- Flaky test: same test passed in another job on same commit → investigate
- PR-caused failure: everything else → fix it

There is no "pre-existing" category. Ban the phrases "pre-existing",
"probably pre-existing", and "likely unrelated" as conclusions.

Add evidence requirement for flaky classification: must show same test
passing in another job on the same commit before calling it flaky.
Reference /investigate-flaky-test skill for flaky test diagnosis.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add analyze-before-fix rule: draft commit message before writing code

Root cause documentation is consistently skipped when it comes after the
code fix — the fix feels like completion and anything planned for "after"
gets dropped. Structural fix: write the analysis first.

- CLAUDE.md: add "Analyze Before Fix" rule — draft commit message with
  root cause before writing the code fix; if planning repo is available,
  write pitfall entry there too
- skills/pr-fix-tests/SKILL.md: add ordering rule at Step 6 — draft
  commit message before coding the fix
- analyses/pr-5431-pitfall-ordering.md: filed postmortem

commit.message:
Revert edge runtime on ingest route

Edge function exceeds Vercel free tier size limit (2.72 MB > 2 MB).
Video ingestion on Vercel is blocked by YouTube anyway — use localhost
or Claude Desktop MCP for ingestion instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: collection amount mismatch, XP not proccing, core popup style

1. Collection: save per-mine coin amounts in mineCoinsMap BEFORE updating
   last_collected. Fixes partial collection where backend recalculated
   after timestamp was already reset.

2. XP: reuse saved mineCoinsMap for XP calculation instead of recalculating
   from mine.last_collected (which was already set to now). Fixes XP
   always being 0 because elapsed time was 0.

3. Core popup: uses openPopup visual style (hq-stat-card rows, styled
   buttons) but renders in #core-detail-overlay on top of mine popup.

refactor: add .mli for h2_lite/hpack module (#35)

* refactor: add .mli for h2_lite/hpack module

Document the public API contract for the 981-line HPACK implementation
(RFC 7541). Exposes types, context management, encoding/decoding,
table lookup, integer/string coding, gRPC helpers, pre-encoded fast
paths, and the Huffman sub-module. Internal functions (evict,
static hash tables, Pre_encoded) remain hidden.

commit.message:
fix: badges move strictly in gap channels, no overlap, smart spawn

1. Remove edge streets — only internal CSS Grid gap channels (h, v1,
   v2) so badges never appear on top of sections.
2. Smart initialization: two-lane spawn (forward+backward) with
   proper spacing so badges never start overlapping.
3. Minimum 20px gap (~3 chars) between badge edges enforced in
   collision detection using actual badge widths.
4. Intersection blocking checks wider radius to prevent pile-ups.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add triple coin (f1·α, f2·α, e·α) as coin_type=5

Completes the 3D basis: f1⊥e, f2⊥e, and e itself. The e·α coin
is the third spatial direction but does not anticommute with τ.

Result: v ≈ 0.521 constant across all sigmas — effectively massless
with a different speed of light. The e·α coin changes speed but
provides no mass gap, same as the β coin.

Summary of all coin types tested:
  0 (β only):      v≈0.966, massless
  1 (e·α only):    v≈0.802, weak mass at intermediate scales
  3 (f1·α+f2·α):   v→0.037, mass at intermediate scales, screens at long λ
  4 (f1+f2+β):     v increases, asymmetric, no help
  5 (f1+f2+e·α):   v≈0.521, massless

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
